---
title: "Project Report"
author: "Dominic Wooldridge, Shane Waldron, Ben Robinson, Daniel Kwong, Mason Brehmer"
date: today
execute:
    warning: false
    message: false
format:
  html:
    theme: darkly
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
---

# Analysis

# Setup + Load Data

```{python}
# =========================
# Full setup chunk (with robust HHInc binning)
# =========================
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, roc_curve, auc

# 1) Load
df = pd.read_csv("df_prepped.csv")

# 2) Standardize column names
df.columns = (
    df.columns.str.strip()
    .str.lower()
    .str.replace(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", regex=True)
    .str.strip("_")
)

# 3) Cleanup
df = df.loc[:, ~df.columns.str.contains(r"^unnamed", case=False)]
df = df.drop(columns=["hh_id"], errors="ignore")

# Optional drops you requested previously
df = df.drop(columns=["dinexp", "fage", "fbirth", "mbirth", "mage"], errors="ignore")

# 4) Ensure bool columns are numeric
bool_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=["bool"]).columns
df[bool_cols] = df[bool_cols].astype(int)

# 5) Robust HHInc binning into: 0-4, 4-9, 9-14
# Works whether df has raw 'hhinc' or one-hot 'hhinc_*'
hhinc_dummies = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("hhinc_")]

if "hhinc" in df.columns:
    df["hhinc"] = pd.to_numeric(df["hhinc"], errors="coerce")
    df["hhinc_bin"] = pd.cut(
        df["hhinc"],
        bins=[0, 4, 9, 14],
        labels=["0-4", "4-9", "9-14"],
        include_lowest=True,
        right=True
    )
    df = df.drop(columns=["hhinc"], errors="ignore")

elif hhinc_dummies:
    tmp = df[hhinc_dummies].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce").fillna(0)

    # If rows are one-hot encoded, this retrieves the active category.
    # If multiple are 1, idxmax picks the first max column.
    chosen = tmp.idxmax(axis=1).str.extract(r"hhinc_(\d+)")[0]
    chosen = pd.to_numeric(chosen, errors="coerce")

    df["hhinc_bin"] = pd.cut(
        chosen,
        bins=[0, 4, 9, 14],
        labels=["0-4", "4-9", "9-14"],
        include_lowest=True,
        right=True
    )

    # Drop old one-hot income columns so model uses bins only
    df = df.drop(columns=hhinc_dummies, errors="ignore")

else:
    # keep workflow running even if no income source found
    df["hhinc_bin"] = pd.Series([pd.NA] * len(df), dtype="object")

# 6) Create binary target: top 25% YogExp = high spender
if "yogexp" not in df.columns:
    raise ValueError("Column 'yogexp' is missing from dataset.")

yog_cut = df["yogexp"].quantile(0.75)
df["high_yogexp"] = (df["yogexp"] >= yog_cut).astype(int)

# 7) Quick checks
print("Shape:", df.shape)
print("YogExp cutoff:", yog_cut)
print("Class balance (high_yogexp mean):", round(df["high_yogexp"].mean(), 4))
print("\nHHInc bin counts:")
print(df["hhinc_bin"].value_counts(dropna=False))
print("\nPreview:")
print(df.head(3))


```

```{python}
# Add this right after loading/cleaning df
df = df.drop(columns=["dinexp","fage", "fbirth", "mbirth", "mage"], errors="ignore")
```

```{python}
# =========================
# 2) Pipeline + classification
# =========================
drop_cols = ["yogexp", "high_yogexp"]  # remove target + leakage
X = df.drop(columns=drop_cols, errors="ignore")
y = df["high_yogexp"].astype(int)

num_cols = X.select_dtypes(include=np.number).columns.tolist()
cat_cols = [c for c in X.columns if c not in num_cols]

preprocess = ColumnTransformer([
    ("num", Pipeline([("imp", SimpleImputer(strategy="median"))]), num_cols),
    ("cat", Pipeline([
        ("imp", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("oh", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore"))
    ]), cat_cols)
])

rf_clf = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocess),
    ("rf", RandomForestClassifier(
        n_estimators=500,
        random_state=42,
        n_jobs=-1,
        class_weight="balanced"
    ))
])

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X, y, test_size=0.25, random_state=42, stratify=y
)

rf_clf.fit(X_train, y_train)

y_pred = rf_clf.predict(X_test)
y_prob = rf_clf.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred))

```


```{python}
# =========================
# 3) ROC curve
# =========================
fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_test, y_prob)
roc_auc = auc(fpr, tpr)

print("AUC:", round(roc_auc, 4))

plt.figure(figsize=(7, 5))
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"RF YogExp (AUC={roc_auc:.3f})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", label="Baseline")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("ROC Curve: High YogExp")
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

```


```{python}
# =========================
# 4) Feature importance
# =========================
feature_names = rf_clf.named_steps["prep"].get_feature_names_out()
importances = rf_clf.named_steps["rf"].feature_importances_

fi_yog = (
    pd.DataFrame({"feature": feature_names, "importance": importances})
    .sort_values("importance", ascending=False)
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

top_n = 20
print(fi_yog.head(top_n))

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plot_df = fi_yog.head(top_n).iloc[::-1]
plt.barh(plot_df["feature"], plot_df["importance"])
plt.title("Top Feature Importance: High YogExp (RF)")
plt.xlabel("Importance")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

```
